 {"id":"bd-36mc","title":"Fix interactive TUI newline drift (raw mode requires CRLF)","description":"# Goal
Fix interactive TUI newline drift in raw mode by aligning CR/LF handling and cursor/viewport calculations.

# Symptoms / Repro
- In raw mode, Enter/Shift+Enter can produce visible drift (cursor offsets, missing/extra blank lines).
- Drift accumulates after multi-line input, paste, or window resize.

# Scope
- Normalize incoming newline sequences for rendering (CRLF in raw mode) while keeping stored text as \n.
- Ensure editor cursor, viewport offsets, and wrapping logic use a single newline representation.
- Apply the same normalization to paste paths, slash-command inserts, and programmatic editor changes.
- Verify compatibility with both text and bash input modes.

# Tests
- Add state/snapshot coverage for multi-line input, paste, and resize with raw mode enabled.
- Regression test for Enter/Shift+Enter cursor positioning and viewport alignment.
- Log before/after cursor position, rendered lines, and input buffer in TestLogger artifacts.

# Acceptance Criteria
- No newline drift after repeated inserts and resizes in raw mode.
- Tests are deterministic and emit JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9.

 {"id":"bd-37a","title":"Implement theme file format and loader","description":"# Implement theme file format and loader

## Goal
Define theme JSON schema and implement theme file loading.

## Implementation

### Theme Types (src/theme.rs)
```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct Theme {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub colors: ThemeColors,
    pub syntax: SyntaxColors,
    pub ui: UiColors,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct ThemeColors {
    pub foreground: String,
    pub background: String,
    pub accent: String,
    pub success: String,
    pub warning: String,
    pub error: String,
    pub muted: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct SyntaxColors {
    pub keyword: String,
    pub string: String,
    pub number: String,
    pub comment: String,
    pub function: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct UiColors {
    pub border: String,
    pub selection: String,
    pub cursor: String,
}
```

### Theme Discovery
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn discover_themes() -> Vec<PathBuf> {
        let mut paths = Vec::new();
        
        // Global themes
        if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {
            let global = home.join(".pi/agent/themes");
            if global.exists() {
                paths.extend(glob_json(&global));
            }
        }
        
        // Project themes
        let project = PathBuf::from(".pi/themes");
        if project.exists() {
            paths.extend(glob_json(&project));
        }
        
        paths
    }
    
    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
        let theme: Theme = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;
        theme.validate()?;
        Ok(theme)
    }
    
    pub fn load_by_name(name: &str) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Search discovered themes for matching name
        for path in Self::discover_themes() {
            if let Ok(theme) = Self::load(&path) {
                if theme.name == name {
                    return Ok(theme);
                }
            }
        }
        Err(Error::ThemeNotFound(name.to_string()))
    }
}
```

### Validation
```rust
impl Theme {
    fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        // Validate all color strings are valid hex
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.foreground)?;
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.background)?;
        // ... etc
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn validate_color(s: &str) -> Result<(), Error> {
        if !s.starts_with('#') || s.len() != 7 {
            return Err(Error::InvalidColor(s.to_string()));
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Themes
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn dark() -> Self {
        Self {
            name: "dark".into(),
            version: "1.0".into(),
            colors: ThemeColors {
                foreground: "#d4d4d4".into(),
                background: "#1e1e1e".into(),
                accent: "#007acc".into(),
                // ...
            },
            // ...
        }
    }
    
    pub fn light() -> Self {
        // Light theme defaults
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Test: Load valid theme JSON
- Test: Reject invalid JSON
- Test: Reject invalid colors
- Test: Discover themes from directories
- Test: Default themes valid

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme struct defined
- [ ] JSON loading works
- [ ] Validation catches bad colors
- [ ] Discovery finds themes
- [ ] Default themes exist","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

 {"id":"bd-37l","title":"Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/anthropic.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Anthropic is the most complex provider implementation:
- Extended thinking support (streaming thinking deltas)
- Cache control headers (ephemeral caching)
- Tool use with JSON schema
- Multiple content block types in responses
- 748 lines of code, 2 unit tests currently

Current reqwest usage (lines to change):
- Line ~200: reqwest::Client creation
- Line ~250: client.post(url).json(&request)
- Line ~280: response.bytes_stream() for SSE
- Various header handling

## Implementation Steps

1. **Replace Client Creation**
```rust
// Before (reqwest)
let client = reqwest::Client::new();

// After (asupersync)
use crate::http::Client;
let client = Client::new()?;
```

2. **Replace Request Building**
```rust
// Before
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send()
    .await?;

// After
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send(&cx)
    .await?;
```

3. **Replace SSE Streaming**
```rust
// Before (reqwest-eventsource or manual)
let mut stream = response.bytes_stream();
while let Some(chunk) = stream.next().await { ... }

// After (asupersync SSE adapter)
use crate::http::SseStream;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);
while let Some(event) = sse.next(&cx).await {
    match event? { ... }
}
```

4. **Update Function Signatures**
```rust
// Add Cx parameter to stream()
async fn stream(
    &self,
    cx: &Cx,  // New parameter
    context: &Context,
    options: &StreamOptions,
) -> Result<...>
```

## Anthropic-Specific Considerations
1. **Extended Thinking**: Must handle thinking_start/delta/end events correctly
2. **Cache Headers**: anthropic-beta header for cache control
3. **Error Responses**: Parse error JSON from non-2xx responses
4. **Rate Limiting**: Respect 429 responses with retry-after header
5. **Content Types**: text, thinking, tool_call blocks in single response

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)

## Testing
- Existing 2 unit tests must still pass
- Add integration test with mock SSE server
- Test extended thinking stream parsing
- Test tool call accumulation

## Files
- src/providers/anthropic.rs (modify ~50 lines)
- src/provider.rs (update trait if Cx needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports in anthropic.rs
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works end-to-end
- [ ] Extended thinking parsing works
- [ ] Tool calls parsed correctly
- [ ] Error handling preserved","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

 {"id":"bd-37z","title":"Spec: Hostcall ABI + capability manifest","description":"# Goal
Define a **precise hostcall ABI** and **capability manifest** so every extension I/O is explicit, auditable, and enforceable.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Request/response schema: `id`, `capability`, `method`, `params`, `timeout_ms`, `cancel_token`.
- Error taxonomy: deterministic mapping for timeouts, denied capability, IO failure.
- Streaming contract: chunked responses + backpressure signaling.
- Capability manifest schema: declarative list of required connectors + scope (paths, hosts, methods).
- Structured log schema: request_id, extension_id, capability, scope, duration, outcome, redaction flags.

# Security Invariants
- No hostcall executes without a granted capability.
- Capability scope is checked before any connector side effect.
- Logs are append-only and redact secrets by default.

# Tests This Enables
- ABI conformance fixtures (unit).
- Capability-deny tests with exact error mapping.
- E2E log assertions using the standardized schema.

# Non-goals
- Implicit global OS access.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:21:53.288452067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:48.887495033Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:41.049085160Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec+schema+Rust validation)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-37z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-37z. I’ll spec host_call/host_result ABI + capability manifest by updating EXTENSIONS.md (authoritative narrative) and tightening docs/schema/extension_protocol.json for host_call (capability+method+params+timeout+cancel) + host_result (typed error taxonomy + optional streaming fields). Will update src/extensions.rs sample messages/tests accordingly and run cargo check/clippy/fmt/test.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:32:33Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-37z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-37z: Spec: Hostcall ABI + capability manifest

- EXTENSIONS.md: added Hostcall ABI (capability+method+params+timeout_ms+cancel_token), capability derivation rules, error taxonomy, streaming contract, capability manifest schema (pi.ext.cap.v1), and hostcall evidence ledger (params_hash + timing) via pi.ext.log.v1.
- docs/schema/extension_protocol.json: host_call now requires capability/method/params (+ timeout_ms/cancel_token/context); host_result adds typed error + chunk/backpressure metadata and enforces error presence iff is_error=true; added host_call_error_code/error and host_stream_chunk/backpressure defs.
- src/extensions.rs: updated protocol structs + validation; enforces declared capability matches core-derived requirement; validates params/output are objects; validates capability manifest schema; removed panic! macros from tests (UBS).
- benches/extensions.rs + tests/extensions_manifest.rs updated for new host_call ABI and required_capability_for_host_call signature.

Verified: cargo fmt/check/clippy -D warnings/test all pass; ubs --diff --only=rust shows Critical=0.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-385a","title":"Interactive: represent tool calls/results as first-class chat items","description":"# Goal
Refactor interactive conversation rendering so tool calls and tool results are modeled as distinct message/item types.

# Background
Legacy UI can collapse/expand tool output because tool calls/results are separate components.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Extend the in-memory conversation representation to include:
  - user message
  - assistant message
  - assistant thinking block(s)
  - tool call start (name + args)
  - tool result (stdout/stderr/exit + metadata)
  - system notices
- Ensure items can be rendered with consistent styling.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tool calls/results are no longer “just text”; they are structured items.
- [ ] Existing agent event integration feeds these items correctly.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

- Action `toggleThinking` (legacy default Ctrl+T):
  - toggles thinking visibility at runtime.

- Keybinding router (`bd-gze`) for Ctrl+T.
